opus.scenario: ASN quantity mismatch, full evidence on hand

draft.argument_summary: The full 48 units on the ASN were shipped and are documented on the carrier BOL; Amazon received-scanned only 40, so the 8-unit variance is a receiving/scan discrepancy, not a genuine short-ship.
draft.letter_body:
This dispute concerns an ASN Mismatch chargeback assessed against PO-7741AB (ASIN B08N5WRWNW) for $420.00 on amazon.com. We are disputing this deduction in full.

Our records show we shipped the complete quantity of 48 units against the submitted ASN for this PO. Amazon's receiving facility, however, received-scanned only 40 units and deducted the 8-unit difference as a quantity mismatch.

The full shipped quantity is documented across three independent records: (1) the ASN we submitted, which reflects 48 units; (2) our packing list, which itemizes 48 units; and (3) the carrier Bill of Lading, which confirms 48 units tendered to and accepted by the carrier. Because all shipping documentation is consistent at 48 units, the 8-unit shortfall reflected in Amazon's received scan is a receiving or scan discrepancy at the fulfillment center — not a genuine short-ship on our part.

We respectfully request reversal of the full $420.00, as the deducted units were verifiably shipped and tendered to the carrier as reflected on the BOL.
evidence_to_attach: Submitted ASN record showing 48 units for PO-7741AB
Carrier Bill of Lading (BOL) showing 48 units tendered
missing_evidence: Signed proof of delivery (POD) confirming carton/unit count received at the FC
Carton-level detail or pallet manifest tying 48 units to specific carton IDs/tracking
Amazon receive-scan report or ARN showing the 40-unit scan, to pinpoint where the 8 units dropped
draft.filing_note: File within the SCE/dispute window shown on the deduction (typically limited days from the chargeback date). Submit promptly and confirm all three documents are legible and clearly show the 48-unit count.

verdict.rationale: Correctly disputable ASN mismatch/shortage: the dispute demands the receiving FC account for the gap and marshals three consistent shipping records (ASN, packing list, BOL) without inventing any facts. It also honestly flags that a signed POD and the receive-scan/ARN are the missing pieces that would clinch reversal, which is exactly the right strategy for a quantity-received dispute.

opus.scenario: ASN discrepancy vs PO, partial evidence

We are disputing an ASN quantity discrepancy chargeback of $380.00 assessed against PO-7782CD (ASIN B08N5WRWNW) on amazon.com.

The purchase order called for 36 units. Our transmitted ASN reflected 36 units, and the physical shipment contained 36 units, so the ASN, the PO, and the shipped quantity are fully aligned. There is no quantity discrepancy between what was ordered, what was declared in the ASN, and what was shipped.

Because the ASN matched the PO exactly, the basis for this deduction is incorrect. We request a full reversal of the $380.00 chargeback. Our submitted ASN record is attached as supporting evidence, and we are prepared to provide additional confirmation of the received quantity on request.
evidence_to_attach: Submitted ASN record showing 36 units for PO-7782CD
missing_evidence: Amazon receiving report / receipt confirmation showing the quantity actually scanned in at the FC
Bill of lading (BOL) or packing slip showing 36 units shipped
Carrier proof of delivery confirming the shipment/carton count received
draft.filing_note: File within the chargeback dispute window shown in Vendor Central (typically 30 days from the deduction date). Attach the ASN record plus the receiving report as soon as it is available to strengthen the claim.

verdict.rationale: Cleanly grounded in the provided facts (correct PO, ASIN, amount, 36 units) with no fabrication, professional tone, and the right strategy for an ASN-vs-PO discrepancy: assert alignment and, critically, flag the FC receiving report as the missing evidence that actually decides these cases. Only gap is that the argument rests on the ASN alone—the win hinges on obtaining the receiving/POD proof it correctly notes it doesn't yet have—so it's sendable but should be reinforced once that evidence is in hand.

opus.scenario: ASN mismatch, vendor lists no evidence at all

draft.argument_summary: The ASN mismatch flag does not reflect a fulfillment error; the shipment was transmitted and physically shipped in agreement with PO-7810EF, and the mismatch appears to be a data/timing discrepancy rather than a genuine content difference.
draft.letter_body:
We are disputing the ASN mismatch chargeback of $295.00 assessed against PO-7810EF (ASIN B07FZ8S74R) on amazon.com.

Amazon flagged an ASN mismatch on the inbound shipment for this PO. We respectfully dispute this deduction. The ASN for PO-7810EF was transmitted prior to the shipment's arrival, and the units physically shipped correspond to the quantities and ASIN confirmed on the purchase order. An ASN mismatch flag is frequently the result of a data transmission timing issue, a partial or split delivery being reconciled against a single ASN, or a receiving-scan discrepancy at the fulfillment center — none of which represents an actual error in what the vendor shipped.

Based on the shipping and ASN records for this PO, the transmitted ASN accurately reflected the shipment contents. We therefore request a full reversal of the $295.00 deduction. Supporting documentation reconciling the transmitted ASN against the PO and shipment is available upon request.
missing_evidence: Copy of the ASN (856) as transmitted to Amazon, with the transmission timestamp showing it was sent before/at delivery
The confirmed purchase order PO-7810EF showing ordered ASIN and quantities
Bill of lading (BOL) / packing list matching the ASN contents
Carrier proof of delivery / appointment confirmation for the inbound shipment
Amazon's receiving report or discrepancy detail showing exactly what field mismatched (quantity, ASIN, carton count, or ship-to)
draft.filing_note: File within the Vendor Central dispute window (typically 30–60 days from the chargeback issue date) — confirm the exact deadline on the deduction record and submit before it lapses.

verdict.rationale: The letter is well-written and stays close to the source facts, but it asserts as fact ("the transmitted ASN accurately reflected the shipment contents," "ASN was transmitted prior to arrival") things the vendor has zero evidence for — none of the five required documents are on hand. An ASN mismatch is only winnable by producing the actual 856/timestamp and reconciling it against Amazon's discrepancy detail; arguing generic "timing issue" theories with no attachments will be rejected. Correct move is to gather the flagged evidence first (especially Amazon's receiving report showing which field mismatched) rather than send now.

The signed packing list confirms a count of 60 units, and the weight ticket corroborates that the shipped quantity is consistent with the declared unit count. Because the ASN, the signed packing list, and the weight ticket all agree at 60 units, any variance recorded at the receiving dock reflects a receiving or reconciliation discrepancy rather than an ASN inaccuracy on our part.

opus.scenario: Missing FNSKU label — the model must not manufacture a winning dispute

draft.argument_summary: The vendor's own account confirms the prep service missed FNSKU labels on some units, so the deduction reflects a genuine compliance failure and is not realistically winnable.
draft.letter_body:
Re: PO-7768IJ — Chargeback for missing FNSKU label on units (ASIN B09B8V1LZ3), deducted amount $160.00 on amazon.com.

We have reviewed this chargeback. Our records confirm that our prep service missed applying FNSKU labels on a subset of units in this shipment. We acknowledge that the labeling requirement was not fully met at the unit level for those units.

The only evidence available to us is a photograph showing correctly applied FNSKU labels on other units in the shipment. This demonstrates that labels were applied to some units, but it does not establish that every unit in the deducted quantity was compliant, and therefore does not rebut the basis of the chargeback.

We are submitting this for review and will implement corrective action with our prep provider to ensure full FNSKU coverage on future shipments. If Amazon has documentation identifying the specific affected units, we would appreciate receiving it so we can reconcile against our records.
evidence_to_attach: Photo of correctly applied FNSKU label on other units in the shipment
missing_evidence: Photos showing FNSKU labels applied to EVERY unit in the deducted quantity
Prep service certification or attestation confirming 100% FNSKU labeling was completed for PO-7768IJ
Amazon's receiving documentation identifying the specific unit count deemed unlabeled
Reconciliation of shipped unit quantity vs. quantity flagged as missing labels
draft.filing_note: Chargeback disputes must generally be filed within 30 days of the deduction posting to Vendor Central. Because the vendor's own account admits the labels were missed, expect a low approval likelihood — prioritize corrective action with the prep provider over litigating this deduction.

verdict.rationale: This correctly identifies a labeling chargeback where the vendor is at fault as non-disputable, honestly concedes the shortfall rather than manufacturing a false win, and uses only the provided facts. The right strategy for a legitimate missing-FNSKU deduction is exactly this: acknowledge, commit to corrective action, and request Amazon's receiving detail to reconcile — no invented POs, amounts, or dates.

We acknowledge that "not scannable at the FC" is a receiving-side scan result, and we are not disputing that a scan issue was recorded. We are requesting review to confirm whether the labels as-produced conformed to the required specification and, if so, that the deduction be reversed. If the labels are confirmed non-conforming, we will address the root cause with our labeling process.
missing_evidence: Photos of the actual labels as applied to the units/cartons shipped on PO-7799KL
Proof the printed barcode meets scan-grade/verification standards (e.g., a barcode verifier report showing grade)
Quantity/percentage of units flagged as not scannable vs. total shipped, to check the deduction math against $140.00
Any FC scan-failure detail or defect notice tying the failure to specific units on this PO

verdict.rationale: Well-grounded and honestly framed — it invents no facts and correctly acknowledges that a "not scannable at FC" scan result is a receiving-side verdict that's very hard to overturn, especially with only a label spec and no verifier report or as-applied label photos. It's the right posture (labeling chargebacks rarely win), but as-is it isn't a compelling reversal case: a spec alone proves intended format, not that the actual printed/applied labels scanned to grade. Hold until the flagged evidence (verifier report, photos, defect detail, quantity math) is obtained.

## Chargeback Dispute: Quantity Discrepancy – PO-8001AB

We are disputing the $145.00 deduction assessed against purchase order PO-8001AB for ASIN B07PGL2N7J due to an alleged quantity discrepancy.

### Summary of Facts

Amazon deducted $145.00 from our account citing a quantity discrepancy on this PO. However, our packing list confirms that the correct quantity of units was packed, sealed, and shipped to Amazon's fulfillment center. There is no evidence of shortage on our end at time of shipment.

### Why This Deduction Is Invalid

A quantity discrepancy chargeback is valid only when a carrier damages goods in transit, units are genuinely missing upon FBA receipt, or our records demonstrably show undershipment. Our packing list provides documentation that the ordered quantity was prepared and shipped in full. Without Amazon providing specific evidence of units missing at inbound scan, this deduction lacks proper foundation.

We request that Amazon review inbound scan records and reconcile this against our documentation. If units were lost or damaged in carrier custody, that is a carrier claim, not a vendor liability.

### Requested Resolution

Please reverse the $145.00 deduction and provide visibility into the specific units claimed as short.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.
missing_evidence: Shipment receipt confirmation or carrier manifest showing date/time delivered to Amazon
Inbound scan report from Amazon showing units received vs. units claimed short
Any correspondence from Amazon itemizing which SKUs or units were marked as discrepant
draft.filing_note: File this dispute within 14 days of the chargeback date. If Amazon does not respond within 30 days, escalate to Vendor Manager. Gather inbound scan records and carrier documentation proactively to strengthen future rebuttals.

As drafted it leans on the packing list and asks Amazon to do the reconciliation, so it isn't sendable until the flagged POD/carrier and inbound-scan evidence is actually obtained and attached.
